threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think the Tube is just a convenient way to get around, but you have no idea it’s a social experiment in patience and chaos.
Last night, saw a couple arguing over the last slice of pizza on the 47. They ended up sharing it with a random bloke who joined in, classic London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when I heard a toddler arguing with their parent over who gets the last slice of pizza from a corner shop. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM and still hear the spontaneous techno beats from an open-window party nearby.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they avoid the Civic Center BART entrance—it's like a game of dodgeball with the delays and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 12 at 4th street today, and it was packed with a marching band in full costume. Only in SF do you see such random joy on a commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Many tourists think Gràcia is just all about the Gaudí hype, but the real charm is sneaking into hidden plazas for a quiet coffee among locals.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll catch them staring blankly at the tram timetable, like it's an ancient scroll, while locals breeze past without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stop at the traffic lights on Dalston Junction, waiting for a green man. Locals just cross wherever, like it’s a game of hopscotch.
Caught a glimpse of a couple on the 59 sharing a kebab like it was a romantic feast. Only in Brixton could a night bus feel so special.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last summer, I watched a guy juggle flaming torches outside the Reuterplatz while kids danced around him. That's when it hit me: this place is magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a beer after midnight at a Späti while debating the best Döner kebab joint with a stranger. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals know to dodge the BART Civic Center station after the latest loud bang. Tourists still standing around confused, phones out.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 one evening, packed with locals and tourists alike, all jamming to a street musician. Only in SF do you get a full concert on your way home.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into any bar for tapas at 8 PM. Real locals know that's when the kitchen closes, and we're just getting started.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their over-reliance on Google Maps for directions. Locals navigate by landmarks, not just the shortest route.
